explanations.1: Flow rate is defined as volume per unit time, so its SI unit is cubic meters per second.
explanations.2: Using $v = Q/A$, where $Q = 5.00 \times 10^{-4} m^3/s$ and $A = \pi(0.009 m)^2$, the velocity is approximately 1.96 m/s.
explanations.3: Since the product of area and velocity must remain constant ($A_1v_1 = A_2v_2$), increasing the cross-sectional area causes the speed to decrease.
explanations.4: By the equation of continuity, reducing the cross-sectional area of the opening causes the fluid velocity to increase significantly.
explanations.5: Bernoulli’s principle refers to the relationship where pressure drops as speed increases for a fluid at a constant height ($h_1 = h_2$).
explanations.6: All terms in Bernoulli's equation have units of pressure ($N/m^2$), which is dimensionally equivalent to energy per unit volume ($J/m^3$).
explanations.7: High-velocity air between the vehicles creates a region of lower pressure compared to the outside, resulting in a net force pushing the vehicles together.
explanations.8: Entrainment occurs when a high-velocity fluid creates a low-pressure area that draws another fluid into the stream, as seen in Bunsen burners or atomizers.
explanations.9: If resistance is negligible, the speed $v = \sqrt{2gh}$ depends only on the depth of the opening below the surface, not the size of the opening itself.
explanations.10: Multiplying the pressure (energy per unit volume) by the flow rate (volume per unit time) gives the power associated with the pressure of the fluid.
explanations.12: Once the fluid emerges into the atmosphere, it is surrounded by the atmosphere and its absolute pressure must equal atmospheric pressure.
explanations.13: Laminar flow is characterized by smooth, non-mixing layers of fluid following continuous streamlines.
explanations.14: Resistance is inversely proportional to the fourth power of the radius ($1/r^4$), so doubling $r$ decreases resistance by $2^4 = 16$.
explanations.15: The SI unit for viscosity is Pascal-seconds ($Pa \cdot s$), which can also be written as $N \cdot s/m^2$.
explanations.16: The relationship $P_2 - P_1 = RQ$ describes how pressure drops due to resistance and flow rate.
explanations.17: Flow rate is proportional to $r^4$; since $(0.95)^4 \approx 0.81$, the flow is 81% of original, which is a 19% reduction.
explanations.18: Although individual capillaries are tiny, the massive number of them creates a huge total cross-sectional area, which decreases velocity via $Q = Av$.
explanations.19: The characteristic aerofoil shape causes air to move faster over the top surface than the bottom, leading to lower pressure on top and a net upward lift.
explanations.20: This term represents the kinetic energy per unit volume multiplied by the volume flow rate, yielding power.
